original_description: Upload local images to a GitHub PR and embed them in the description or comments. Use when asked to "attach screenshots to PR", "add images to PR", "upload test results to PR", "embed screenshots in PR description", "add before/after images to PR", "attach UI screenshots", "show test results in PR", "add visual evidence to PR", or any request involving images and PRs. Always use this skill when the user wants to visually document changes in a pull request, even if they don't use the word "upload" — phrases like "put the screenshot in the PR" or "show the image in the PR" should trigger this skill.
best_description: Embed a local image file (screenshot, .png/.jpg/.gif, CleanShot capture, before/after, UI capture, CI failure image) into an existing GitHub pull request — either in the PR body or as a PR comment. **The GitHub API and `gh` CLI do not support image uploads**, so you cannot do this with `gh pr edit` or `gh pr comment` alone; this skill is the only way to get a local image into a PR, and it works by driving a real browser to GitHub's user-attachments uploader. Use whenever a message references both (a) a local image — by file path, filename, "screenshot", "image", "screen recording", "cleanshot", or location like Desktop/Downloads/tmp — AND (b) an existing pull request — by number (#1234), URL, branch name ("the PR for branch X"), or phrases like "the open PR" or "the PR I just opened". The verb does not matter (upload, attach, embed, add, put, post, drop, slap, stick, include, show, document, "can this go on", "get this onto"). Also use for visually documenting test runs, bug repros, UI states, or CI failures inside an existing PR. Do NOT use for: capturing or taking a new screenshot, editing/resizing images, opening a brand-new PR (use the /pr skill), pushing branches, or referencing images inside repo files like README.md.
final_description: Use this skill when a user wants a local image file to end up visible inside an existing GitHub pull request. Trigger whenever the message names or implies a local image (a path, screenshot, .png/.jpg/.gif, CleanShot file, before/after pair, UI capture, CI failure capture, or anything in Desktop/Downloads/tmp/~) AND references an existing PR (by #number, URL, branch name, "the open PR", "the PR I just opened", "the PR comments"). The intent matters, not the verb: attach, embed, add, upload, put, post, drop, slap, stick, include, show, document, "can this go on", "get this onto" — all qualify, whether destination is PR body or comment, one image or many. Also use for visually documenting bug repros, test runs, UI states, before/after diffs, or CI failures in an existing PR. This is the only working path; `gh` and the GitHub API cannot upload images. Do NOT use for: capturing new screenshots, editing/resizing images, opening a new PR, pushing branches, or referencing images already committed in the repo.

history.description: Embed a local image file (screenshot, .png/.jpg/.gif, CleanShot capture, before/after, UI capture, CI failure image) into an existing GitHub pull request — either in the PR body or as a PR comment. **The GitHub API and `gh` CLI do not support image uploads**, so you cannot do this with `gh pr edit` or `gh pr comment` alone; this skill is the only way to get a local image into a PR, and it works by driving a real browser to GitHub's user-attachments uploader. Use whenever a message references both (a) a local image — by file path, filename, "screenshot", "image", "screen recording", "cleanshot", or location like Desktop/Downloads/tmp — AND (b) an existing pull request — by number (#1234), URL, branch name ("the PR for branch X"), or phrases like "the open PR" or "the PR I just opened". The verb does not matter (upload, attach, embed, add, put, post, drop, slap, stick, include, show, document, "can this go on", "get this onto"). Also use for visually documenting test runs, bug repros, UI states, or CI failures inside an existing PR. Do NOT use for: capturing or taking a new screenshot, editing/resizing images, opening a brand-new PR (use the /pr skill), pushing branches, or referencing images inside repo files like README.md.

I just finished testing the search refactor. There are 3 screenshots in /tmp/search-test/ — before.png, after-empty.png, and after-results.png. Put them on the open PR for the search-fix branch please.

history.description: Use this skill whenever the user wants a local image (screenshot, PNG/JPG/GIF, CleanShot capture, before/after, UI capture, CI failure, test result) to appear on an existing GitHub pull request — in the PR body or as a comment. Trigger on any image reference (file path, filename, "screenshot", "image", "cleanshot", Desktop/Downloads/tmp/~ location) combined with an existing PR reference (#number, URL, "the PR", "the open PR", "the PR for branch X", "the PR I just opened"). Any verb counts: upload, attach, embed, add, put, post, drop, slap, stick, include, show, document, "get this onto", "can this go on". Also covers visually documenting bug repros, test runs, UI states, before/after comparisons, or CI failures inside an existing PR. The `gh` CLI cannot do this alone — this skill is the only path. Do NOT use for: taking new screenshots, editing/resizing images, opening a brand-new PR, pushing branches, or referencing images inside committed repo files.

history.description: Use when a user wants a local image file to appear inside an existing GitHub pull request. The signature is two ingredients in one ask: (1) a local image — file path, screenshot, .png/.jpg/.gif, CleanShot capture, before/after pair, UI capture, CI failure, or anything in Desktop/Downloads/tmp/~ — and (2) an existing PR — by #number, URL, branch ("PR for branch X"), or phrasing like "the open PR" or "the PR I just opened". Trigger regardless of verb (attach, embed, add, upload, put, post, drop, slap, include, show, document, "can this go on", "get this onto"), destination (PR body or comment), or count (one image or several). Also covers visually documenting test runs, bug repros, UI states, before/after diffs, or CI failures in an existing PR. This is the only path — `gh` and the GitHub API can't upload images. Skip for: taking a new screenshot, editing/resizing images, opening a brand-new PR, pushing branches, or referencing images already committed in the repo.

history.description: Use this skill whenever a request pairs a local image with an existing GitHub pull request — the user wants the image visible inside that PR. Image cues: a file path, filename, screenshot, .png/.jpg/.gif, CleanShot, before/after pair, UI capture, CI failure capture, or a file in Desktop/Downloads/tmp/~. PR cues: #number, URL, branch ("PR for branch X"), "the open PR", "the PR I just opened", "the PR comments". Any verb qualifies — attach, embed, add, upload, put, post, drop, slap, stick, include, show, document, "can this go on", "get this onto". One image or many; PR body or comment; both count. Also use when the user wants to visually document a bug repro, test run, UI state, before/after, or CI failure on an existing PR. This is the only path that works — `gh` and the GitHub API cannot upload images. Do NOT use for: capturing a new screenshot, editing/resizing images, opening a brand-new PR, pushing branches, or referencing images already committed in the repo.
